summary:
Oral endotracheal intubation is less effective in helicoper environments due to poor visibility from motion-induced vibrations and limited access to resources like proper lighting and skilled staff. This leads to increased difficulty during procedures requiring precise visual assessment and coordination among team members under unstable conditions.

The summary provided by Agent1 correctly identifies critical factors affecting intubation performance—specifically environmental instability, lack of optimal lighting, absence of adequate staffing, and diminished procedural precision—that collectively impair effectiveness when performing this procedure outside controlled hospital facilities.
